preamble: Monophthongization of 'oi' to 'u' involves the simplification of a rounded diphthong into a high back vowel.

examples.note: Latin diphthongal 'oe/oi' simplified to 'u' in Classical Latin. E.g., archaic 'oinos' > classical 'unus'.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
